rationale: Re-review after the DI-22 amendment (block-style satisfies handling + safe text embedding, fd78dd2). All twelve probes executed via rdm story mutation-probe against test_new_input_scaffolds_a_traced_design_input; all KILLED, none survived. Clause table: (1) next unused DI id across the whole DHF -- return 'DI-2' instead of max+1: KILLED (fixture takes DI-1/DI-3 across two contexts; test demands exactly {DI-1, DI-3, DI-4}). (2) entry inserted into the CHOSEN context's design_inputs -- doc = contexts[sorted(contexts)[0]]: KILLED. Note: this exact mutant was documented as behaviorally equivalent in the previous review (single scaffold into the sorted-first context); the strengthened test's second scaffold into 'trends' now kills it. (3) stub tagged with the new id -- @allure.story('DI-TODO'): KILLED; stub FAILS until implemented -- pytest.fail(...) -> pass: KILLED by the pytest.ExitCode.TESTS_FAILED assertion on a real pytest run of the stub. (4) checklist printed -- reword the CHECKLIST header so 'checklist' vanishes from stdout: KILLED. (5) unknown context rejected -- 'if context not in contexts:' -> 'if False:': KILLED; unknown user need rejected -- 'if unknown:' -> 'if False:': KILLED (backed by the ids-unchanged assertion, nothing scaffolded). (6) newly referenced need joins an INLINE satisfies list -- rewrite the inline list without the added refs: KILLED by 'satisfies: [UN-001, UN-002]' in alarms.md. (7) AMENDED: a BLOCK-style satisfies list is edited in place without a duplicate key -- (a) disable the block-branch guard so the code falls through to the create-new-key path: KILLED by trends_text.count('satisfies:') == 1 (the duplicate-key assertion); (b) misplace the appended item at the closing fence (end + offset -> close + offset) so it lands outside the list: KILLED by the parsed frontmatter['satisfies'] == ['UN-001', 'UN-002'] assertion. (8) AMENDED: requirement text embedded safely -- (a) _docstring_escape -> identity: KILLED; the hostile text containing a triple-quote corrupts the generated stub and ast.parse raises SyntaxError; (b) _yaml_quote -> bare quoting with no escaping: KILLED by the exact YAML round-trip assertion declared['DI-5']['text'] == hostile. No surviving mutants; the previously recorded equivalent mutant (sorted-first context) is now killed by the strengthened test.

probes.find: pytest.fail("{di_id} acceptance test not implemented -- replace this stub with real assertions")
